s: First to move — Aries is read as the initiator, acting before the plan is finished and treating hesitation as the only real failure.
Direct address — the sign is ascribed a bluntness that skips negotiation and states the want plainly.
Competitive nerve — tradition credits Aries with a need to test itself against something, converting a room into a contest without announcing it.
Fast recovery — Aries is read as quick to leave a setback behind, treating defeat as data rather than verdict.
Physical courage — the Cardinal Fire placement is described as comfortable stepping toward risk that others are still assessing.
d: Unfinished ventures — the tradition notes a pattern of momentum spent on the start, with the follow-through left for someone else.
Impatience read as contempt — Aries's need for speed is described as reading, to slower parties, like dismissal.
Combat where none was called for — the competitive instinct is said to surface even in exchanges that were not contests.
Blunt force — the directness ascribed to Aries is described as landing as injury when the moment called for tact.
Short attention for consequence — the sign is read as acting on impulse before the cost of the act has been priced in.
Aries.m: The Aries drive is described as the need to act first and prove itself through the act — being early matters more to this pattern than being right.
Aries.g: The classic counsel for the Aries pattern is patience it did not ask for — the tradition reads its work as learning to let a thing finish before starting the next, and to let a win count even when it was slow.
Aries.l: In partnership, the Aries pattern is described as pursuing directly and losing interest once the chase resolves into routine. The tradition reads it as needing a partner who can meet its pace without being run over by it, and as most stable when the relationship itself is treated as an ongoing contest worth winning rather than a settled prize.
Aries.es: Cardinal fire, the sign of first ignition. Aries is read as the point where undifferentiated will becomes a single directed act — the archetype of beginning, not of arriving.
gi: Momentum. Aries is read as the sign that turns a stalled room by simply beginning, before consensus is reached.
Directness. The tradition credits Aries with a refusal to disguise intent — desire and action are read as the same motion.
Unearned courage. Aries is granted the nerve to enter unmapped territory, not because the danger is assessed, but because hesitation is foreign to it.
Combative honesty. The archetype is read as incapable of the polite lie; conflict, in this reading, is simply truth arriving early.
Aries.ha: The tradition holds that Aries starts more than it finishes, and that its fire runs hottest at the threshold and coolest by the middle of any long task. What it ignites, it is rarely present to tend.
Aries.fr: Aries is read as a friend built through shared risk rather than shared confidence — the tradition bonds it fastest to those who move at speed and forgives it fastest for the friendships it forgets to maintain.

va.label: First decan · Mars
va.note: Mars doubled: the tradition reads this ten degrees as Aries at its most unmixed — combative, immediate, first.

va.label: Second decan · Sun
va.note: Solar influence is said to add self-possession, turning raw initiation toward a more deliberate, sovereign will.

va.label: Third decan · Venus
va.note: Venus is read as softening the edge, lending the final decan a taste for charm and alliance alongside the charge forward.

Aries.wk: The Aries pattern is read as built for the opening move — the task that has stalled and needs someone to simply begin. Its labor runs hottest at the threshold and thins by the middle of any long stretch, so the tradition assigns it the sprint rather than the vigil. Work that calls for a fast, unhesitating start suits it; work that asks it to tend what it has already lit is where the pattern is read to wander.
Aries.cr: In conflict the Aries pattern is read as stating the grievance directly and early, treating the quarrel itself as truth arriving ahead of schedule rather than as damage. The tradition holds it cools as fast as it flares — the repair it favors is the clean, immediate word and a quick return to level, not a slow accounting. What mends it is a counterpart willing to have the disagreement plainly and let it end.

s: Load-bearing patience — Taurus is read as the sign that finishes what it starts, valuing the slow accumulation of a thing built to last.
Sensory groundedness — the Venus rulership is ascribed a comfort with the physical world, food, touch, and material pleasure taken seriously rather than as indulgence.
Reliability under pressure — the Fixed Earth placement is described as holding its position when conditions get unstable.
Loyalty once committed — tradition credits Taurus with a durability of attachment that outlasts the initial appeal.
Practical judgment — the sign is read as slow to decide but rarely wrong once it has, favoring the tested over the novel.
d: Resistance to change — the tradition notes a stubbornness that holds a position past the point it still serves the sign.
Possessiveness — Taurus's attachment to what it has built is described as tipping into a refusal to share or release it.
Slow to arrive — the sign's caution is read as costing it opportunities that required a faster answer.
Comfort mistaken for growth — Taurus is described as settling into what is pleasant even when the pleasant has stopped serving it.
Immovability read as indifference — the fixed temperament is said to be misread by others as not caring, when the sign is simply not moving yet.
Taurus.m: The Taurus drive is described as the need for durable security — what is built, tasted, or held matters to this pattern more than what is merely promised.
Taurus.g: The tradition reads Taurus's maturation as learning to distinguish the comfortable from the necessary — the counsel ascribed to the sign is to release what has stopped growing rather than defend it out of habit.
Taurus.l: The Taurus pattern in partnership is described as slow to commit and, once committed, resistant to letting go. Tradition reads it as expressing affection through steadiness and physical presence rather than declaration, and as most secure in a bond that does not ask it to move faster than its own pace allows.
Taurus.es: Fixed earth, the sign of held ground. Taurus is read as matter's insistence on continuing to exist exactly as it is — the archetype of duration, not of change.
gi: Endurance. The tradition credits Taurus with a patience that outlasts the crisis, holding a position long after urgency has moved on to other signs.
Sensory fidelity. Taurus is read as the guardian of the material world's pleasures — texture, taste, and quiet, unhurried comfort are treated as legitimate ends in themselves.
Reliability. The archetype is granted a constancy others are said to set their own plans against, a fixed point in a chart otherwise full of motion.
Groundedness. Taurus is read as the sign that keeps abstraction honest, returning any idea to the question of whether it can actually be built, held, or eaten.
Taurus.ha: The tradition holds that Taurus mistakes stillness for safety and resists the very changes that would preserve what it is trying to protect. Its patience, unmanaged, becomes an entrenchment.
Taurus.fr: Taurus is read as a slow-built, rarely broken bond — the tradition ties it to friends earned over long acquaintance rather than instant rapport, and warns that its loyalty, once withdrawn, does not return.

va.label: First decan · Mercury
va.note: Mercury is said to lend the opening decan a practical, detail-minded cast — earth applied to craft and reckoning.

va.label: Second decan · Moon
va.note: The Moon's influence is read into a deepened domesticity, tying this third of the sign to home, memory, and the tending of what already exists.

va.label: Third decan · Saturn
va.note: Saturn is read as hardening resolve, making the final decan the most stubborn and the most enduring of the three.

Taurus.wk: The Taurus pattern is read as built for the long, unhurried finish — the task that favors steady accumulation over speed and asks to be built once, to last. The tradition holds its labor keeps its pace regardless of pressure, slow to begin and slower to abandon, returning to the same ground until the thing is whole. Work that punishes haste and calls for endurance suits it; work that keeps changing shape underneath it is where the pattern is read to dig in rather than adapt.
Taurus.cr: Crossed, the Taurus pattern is read as going still rather than loud — holding its position in a silence others can misread as not caring, when the sign is simply not yet moved. The tradition holds it will not be pushed to a faster reconciliation than its own pace allows; what mends it is patience and unhurried presence rather than argument. Pressed harder it is read to entrench; given time it returns on its own ground.

s: Adaptive intelligence — Gemini is read as the sign that reroutes fastest, treating a change of plan as an opportunity rather than a disruption.
Conversational range — tradition ascribes to Mercury's placement a fluency across subjects and registers, at ease moving between them.
Curiosity as method — the sign is described as gathering information for its own sake, collecting rather than settling on a single answer.
Social versatility — Gemini is read as able to meet nearly any room on its own terms, adjusting tone without apparent effort.
Wit under pressure — the Mutable Air placement is credited with quick verbal recovery when a moment turns unstable.
d: Follow-through deficit — the tradition notes a pattern of interest that migrates before a thing is finished.
Inconsistency read as unreliability — Gemini's changeability is described as costing it trust with those who expect a fixed position.
Surface over depth — the sign is read as gathering breadth of information at the expense of dwelling in any one place long enough to master it.
Restlessness mistaken for evasion — Gemini's need for variety is said to be misread as an unwillingness to commit.
Talk in place of resolution — the tradition ascribes to the sign a tendency to narrate a problem fluently without landing a decision.
Gemini.m: The Gemini drive is described as the need for variety and exchange — what moves, what changes, and what is worth saying about it matters to this pattern more than what stays fixed.
Gemini.g: The classic counsel for Gemini is depth over motion — the tradition reads its work as choosing enough threads to actually finish, rather than adding another before the last one closes.
Gemini.l: In partnership, the Gemini pattern is described as needing conversation and mental stimulation as much as affection, treating a relationship that has stopped talking as one that has stopped working. Tradition reads it as capable of deep attachment but resistant to a bond that forecloses its other interests, most stable when paired with a partner willing to be met on shifting ground.
Gemini.es: Mutable air, the sign of the crossed wire. Gemini is read as intelligence in motion between two points at once — the archetype of exchange, not of settlement.
gi: Fluency. The tradition credits Gemini with a native ease across languages, disciplines, and rooms, translating one context into another without apparent effort.
Curiosity. Gemini is read as incapable of a closed question — the archetype that keeps a subject alive by continually finding one more angle on it.
Adaptability. The tradition grants Gemini a lightness that lets it change position without the weight other signs attach to consistency.
Wit. Gemini is read as the sign that turns information into conversation, valued for the speed and surprise of its connections rather than their depth.
Gemini.ha: The tradition holds that Gemini rarely finishes what it starts examining, and that its dual nature is read literally — as a persistent difficulty in being, or wanting, only one thing at a time.
Gemini.fr: Gemini is read as the archetype of many light bonds rather than few deep ones — the tradition ties it to friends who supply new material to talk about, and it is fastest to fade from company that offers none.

va.label: First decan · Jupiter
va.note: Jupiter is read into an expansive opening decan — curiosity reaching toward larger philosophies and further travel.

va.label: Second decan · Mars
va.note: Mars is said to sharpen the middle decan into argument and debate, air given an edge and a point to press.

va.label: Third decan · Sun
va.note: Solar rulership is read as lending the final decan a performer's polish, communication turned toward audience and display.

Gemini.wk: The Gemini pattern is read as built for work that keeps changing shape — the task that favors quick rerouting, the crossing between subjects, the translation of one context into another. Its labor is fast and mobile, the tradition holds, gathering many threads at once and moving to the next before the last has closed. Work that asks for a single thread held to completion is where the pattern is read to migrate; work that stays various keeps it present.
Gemini.cr: In conflict the Gemini pattern is read as talking — narrating the disagreement fluently from several angles, sometimes circling the problem more readily than landing on it. The tradition holds the repair it trusts is the reopened conversation itself: a bond it can still talk to is one it does not consider broken. What mends it is a counterpart who keeps the exchange live rather than sealing it shut in silence.

s: Instinctive care — Cancer is read as the sign that builds and protects, extending shelter to whoever it has claimed as its own.
Emotional attunement — tradition ascribes to the Moon's rulership a sensitivity that registers what others have not yet said aloud.
Memory as loyalty — the sign is described as holding on to what mattered long after the occasion has passed, and acting from that memory.
Protective resolve — the Cardinal Water placement is credited with an initiating force aimed specifically at defending what it has taken in.
Intuitive reading of a room — Cancer is said to sense mood and undertone before the facts of a situation are stated.
d: Withdrawal under threat — the tradition notes a retreat into a shell when the sign judges the surrounding conditions unsafe.
Mood carried into decision — Cancer's sensitivity is described as swinging its judgment with feelings that have not been examined.
Grudge-holding — the sign's long memory is read as also preserving old injuries past the point they still apply.
Overprotection — Cancer's instinct to shelter is said to tip into a possessiveness that will not let go of what it has claimed.
Indirection under hurt — tradition ascribes to the sign a tendency to signal distress sideways rather than state it, expecting it to be intuited.
Cancer.m: The Cancer drive is described as the need to build and protect a felt sense of home — belonging and emotional safety matter to this pattern more than any external measure of success.
Cancer.g: The tradition reads Cancer's maturation as learning to distinguish caution from safety — the counsel ascribed to the sign is to test the shell's walls occasionally, rather than mistaking withdrawal itself for protection.
Cancer.l: In partnership, the Cancer pattern is described as expressing devotion through care, memory, and the building of a shared domestic world. Tradition reads it as deeply loyal once attachment forms, but prone to withdrawing at the first sign of rejection rather than risking a second one, most stable with a partner who reads its silences as need rather than dismissal.
Cancer.es: Cardinal water, the sign of the built shell. Cancer is read as feeling given a boundary — the archetype of protection, not of exposure.
gi: Memory. The tradition credits Cancer with a retentiveness that treats the past as a living resource, kept close and drawn on rather than discarded.
Nurture. Cancer is read as the sign that feeds and shelters without being asked, extending the instinct for home to whoever is let inside its perimeter.
Attunement. The tradition grants Cancer an unusual sensitivity to undercurrents in a room — what is felt before it is said.
Devotion. Cancer is read as fiercely protective of what it claims as its own, sustaining a bond long after convenience would argue for letting it go.
Cancer.ha: The tradition holds that Cancer's shell built for protection becomes a shell that also confines it, and that its moods move with a tidal regularity it does not fully govern. What it guards, it can also hoard.
Cancer.fr: Cancer is read as a friend who takes in rather than merely knows — the tradition describes it drawing chosen companions into something closer to family, and withdrawing behind its shell at the first sign of being taken lightly.

va.label: First decan · Venus
va.note: Venus is read into the opening decan as an added tenderness, emotional depth expressed through affection and aesthetic care.

va.label: Second decan · Mercury
va.note: Mercury's influence is said to give the middle decan a sharper, more articulate handle on feeling — emotion put into words.

va.label: Third decan · Moon
va.note: The Moon doubled: the tradition reads this final decan as Cancer at its most unmixed — the shell, the tide, and the memory at full strength.

Cancer.wk: The Cancer pattern is read as built for the work of tending — the task done on behalf of something it has claimed to shelter, sustained by memory of why it mattered. Its labor initiates through care rather than assault, the tradition holds, and holds on long after convenience would argue for letting go. Work that asks it to protect and maintain a felt whole suits it; work stripped of any stake worth guarding is where the pattern is read to go cold.
Cancer.cr: Crossed, the Cancer pattern is read as retreating rather than confronting — withdrawing behind its shell and signalling the hurt sideways, expecting it to be intuited rather than stated. The tradition holds the repair depends on the other reading that silence as need rather than dismissal, and drawing the pattern back out before it seals over. What mends it is being met at the shell's edge gently, not pursued through it.

s: Radiant by default — Leo is read as the sign built to be seen, projecting warmth outward without needing to be asked.
Generous at scale — the Leo pattern gives fully once it commits, treating stinginess as beneath it.
Loyal to the circle — Leo defends whoever it has claimed as its own, without qualification.
Creative self-expression — the archetype turns ordinary acts into performance, finding a stage in almost any room.
Steady fire — as a fixed sign, its confidence is read as holding through pressure rather than flaring and dying out.
d: Recognition-dependent — the Leo pattern is read as needing the room's attention to confirm its own worth.
Pride as armor — critique lands as insult, and the tradition notes a reluctance to concede fault.
Center-of-stage drift — the archetype can turn a shared moment into a solo performance without registering the shift.
Fixed stubbornness — once Leo has decided how a thing should go, the tradition reads it as resistant to revision.
Leo.m: What Leo is said to want, before anything else, is to be seen clearly and to matter to the room it is in — recognition read not as vanity but as the confirmation the sign requires in order to keep giving.
Leo.g: The classic counsel for Leo is applause it did not have to ask for — the tradition reads its maturation as learning that the warmth it gives can stand on its own, without a return performance of gratitude.
Leo.l: In relationship, Leo is described as generous and demonstrative, showing devotion openly rather than in private. The archetype is said to court with flourish and to expect a partner's admiration voiced, not merely felt. Where the pattern is read to strain is in sharing the stage — partnership tested by whether Leo can be moved by another's spotlight as readily as its own.
Leo.es: Leo is read as the sign of sovereign display — the self insisting on its own visibility, not as vanity but as a mode of address to a room that must be lit before anyone in it can be seen.
gi: Radiance. Leo is read as the sign that heats a room by simply occupying it, enthusiasm functioning as a public utility rather than a private mood.
Loyalty. The sign's attachments, once formed, the tradition holds run permanent and unconditional; a court once sworn is not easily disbanded.
Generosity. Leo is credited with an open hand — the tradition reads its giving as performed in full view, gift and applause arriving as one gesture.
Nerve. The willingness to stand first and alone before the outcome is known — the tradition's account of the sign's fixed-fire courage.
Leo.ha: The hard road the tradition assigns Leo is the upkeep of the stage: withdrawal of attention reads as a small catastrophe rather than a neutral fact, and the appetite for notice can outlast an audience's patience.
Leo.fr: As friend and ally, Leo is described as fiercely partisan — the tradition holds it defends its circle publicly and expects the same loyalty returned, treating a friend's slight as its own.

va.label: First decan (23 Jul – 2 Aug)
va.note: Ruled by Saturn in the tradition's decan tables — Leo's fire disciplined, the courtier who also keeps the ledger.

va.label: Second decan (3–12 Aug)
va.note: Ruled by Jupiter — the amplifying decan, read as Leo at its most expansive and open-handed.

va.label: Third decan (13–22 Aug)
va.note: Ruled by Mars — the combative decan, the sign's pride sharpened into a readiness to contest.

Leo.wk: The Leo pattern is read as built for work done in full view — the task turned into performance, given its whole warmth once committed, and sustained rather than flared and dropped. As a fixed fire the tradition holds its energy through the long stretch, provided the work is one it can stand behind and be seen doing. Work performed where no one is looking is where the pattern is read to flag; work that is also an address to a room keeps it lit.
Leo.cr: In conflict the Leo pattern is read as taking critique as insult — pride closing over the fault before it can be conceded, the quarrel escalating around dignity rather than the matter itself. The tradition holds it repairs generously once its worth is not in question: a grievance framed without stripping its standing is one it can answer with open warmth. What mends it is respect offered first and correction second.

s: Precision as instinct — Virgo is read as noticing the flaw in the pattern before anyone else does.
Service through competence — the archetype's care is said to take the form of fixing, organizing, improving, rather than declaring itself.
Discernment — Virgo sorts the essential from the excess, the tradition holds, more naturally than it does almost anything else.
Reliability — as a mutable earth sign, it adapts by refining, showing up consistently rather than dramatically.
Modesty — the archetype tends to underclaim its own competence, the tradition notes, rather than perform it.
d: Self-criticism turned inward — the pattern is read as holding itself to a standard it rarely extends to others.
Nitpicking — Virgo's eye for flaws can fix on the trivial, the tradition notes, mistaking correction for care.
Anxious control — order becomes a way of managing worry, and its absence reads to Virgo as threat.
Withholding of praise — the archetype is described as slow to call a thing good enough, even when it is.
Virgo.m: What Virgo is said to want is for things — systems, bodies, days — to work correctly, and it measures its own worth by how much disorder it has managed to reduce.
Virgo.g: The classic counsel for Virgo is completion without correction — the tradition reads its maturation as learning that a thing can be finished and imperfect at once, and that the flaw does not cancel the whole.
Virgo.l: In relationship, Virgo is described as showing devotion through acts of practical care rather than declaration. The archetype is said to relate by attending to detail — remembering the particular, fixing the small thing before it is asked. Where the pattern is read to strain is in the impulse to improve a partner as it would a project, mistaking correction for closeness.
Virgo.es: Virgo is read as the sign of discernment applied to matter — the impulse to sort the usable from the unusable before anything else is permitted to proceed.
gi: Precision. Virgo is credited with an eye that finds the flaw in a pattern others have already approved.
Craft. The tradition reads Virgo's care as work performed on behalf of a whole rather than for individual credit — the repaired system, not the signature on it.
Discretion. A native reticence the tradition treats as a gift in itself — problems handled before they are ever named as problems.
Diagnostic clarity. The sign's analysis is read as clarifying rather than merely critical — the capacity to name exactly where a thing has gone wrong.
Virgo.ha: The recurring difficulty the tradition assigns Virgo is a standard that arrives ahead of the work — completion reads as perpetually deferred, since correction can always find one more correction to make.
Virgo.fr: As friend and ally, Virgo is described as the quietly indispensable one — help arrives practical and unannounced, rarely declared as a favor, which the tradition notes can leave the giving unacknowledged.

va.label: First decan (23 Aug – 1 Sep)
va.note: Ruled by the Sun — Virgo's discernment at its most self-possessed, the critic least in need of an audience.

va.label: Second decan (2–11 Sep)
va.note: Ruled by Venus — the softened decan, precision applied in service of harmony rather than correction alone.

va.label: Third decan (12–22 Sep)
va.note: Ruled by Mercury — Virgo doubled, the decan the tradition reads as the sign's purest analytic register.

Virgo.wk: The Virgo pattern is read as built for the exacting task — the work of refinement, of finding the flaw others approved and setting it right on behalf of the whole rather than the credit. Its labor is meticulous and quietly reliable, the tradition holds, adapting by improving what already exists. The recurring difficulty assigned to it is a standard that arrives ahead of the work, so completion reads as perpetually deferred — there is always one more correction the eye can find.
Virgo.cr: Crossed, the Virgo pattern is read as growing precise rather than loud — naming exactly where a thing went wrong, an accuracy that can land as fault-finding even when it is meant as care. The tradition holds the repair it offers is practical: the small thing fixed before it is asked, correction converted back into service. What mends it is having its diagnosis received as help, and being reminded that a bond need not be corrected to be kept.

s: Diplomatic instinct — Libra is read as the sign that finds the fair account of a dispute before it picks a side.
Aesthetic sense — the archetype is drawn to balance and proportion, in a room as much as in a relationship.
Social fluency — Libra moves easily between perspectives, the tradition holds, translating one party to another.
Partnership orientation — as a cardinal air sign, it initiates through connection, treating the pair as its natural unit.
Fair-mindedness — the archetype weighs competing claims deliberately rather than by instinct or force.
d: Indecision — Libra's weighing of every side is read as a reluctance to close the scale and choose.
Conflict avoidance — the archetype smooths over disagreement, the tradition notes, sometimes before it has been resolved.
Approval-seeking — the pattern is described as shaping itself to match whichever company it is in.
Dependence on partnership — Libra is read as uneasy alone, defining itself against another rather than apart.
Libra.m: What Libra is said to want is equilibrium — a fair balance between self and other, want and obligation — and it treats sustained discord as the condition most worth resolving.
Libra.g: The classic counsel for Libra is a decision made without a second opinion — the tradition reads its maturation as learning to choose and hold a side even where no consensus is available.
Libra.l: In relationship, Libra is described as attentive, accommodating, and oriented toward harmony above nearly any other value. The archetype is said to court through charm and shared aesthetic pleasure, seeking a partner who completes rather than merely joins it. Where the pattern is read to strain is in naming its own want plainly, rather than the one that keeps the peace.
Libra.es: Libra is read as the sign of the weighed decision — not indecision but the insistence that a verdict be earned by holding both sides in view first.
gi: Diplomacy. Libra is credited with the capacity to hold a room's competing claims without collapsing them prematurely into one side.
Aesthetic judgment. The tradition reads Libra as furnishing proportion — the sense of what balances a composition, a table, an argument.
Fair-mindedness. A native reluctance to rule before every party has been heard, read as the sign's contribution to any shared decision.
Sociability. Credited with the ease that turns strangers into company — the scales' native fluency in company itself.
Libra.ha: The hard road the tradition assigns Libra is the cost of scales that never fully settle — a decision deferred in the name of fairness can outlast the situation that required one.
Libra.fr: As friend and ally, Libra is described as the mediator drawn toward the middle of any dispute within its circle, valued for evenness, though the tradition notes that same evenness can read as withheld allegiance once a side must finally be picked.

va.label: First decan (23 Sep – 2 Oct)
va.note: Ruled by the Moon — Libra's balance read through feeling, harmony pursued for its own emotional weather.

va.label: Second decan (3–12 Oct)
va.note: Ruled by Saturn — the sobering decan, fairness held to an almost judicial standard.

va.label: Third decan (13–22 Oct)
va.note: Ruled by Jupiter — the expansive decan, the scales tipped toward generosity and the wider view.

Libra.wk: The Libra pattern is read as built for the weighed decision — the task that asks every side be held in view before a verdict is earned, whether in a dispute, a composition, or a room's competing claims. Its labor is deliberate and even-handed, the tradition holds, furnishing proportion where others rush to a side. The difficulty assigned to it is the scale that never fully settles: a decision deferred in the name of fairness can outlast the situation that required one.
Libra.cr: In conflict the Libra pattern is read as smoothing rather than pressing — reaching for the fair account and the restored calm, sometimes before the disagreement has actually resolved. The tradition holds its truer repair is the harder one: naming its own want plainly instead of the one that keeps the peace. What mends it is a counterpart who makes it safe to disagree, so the balance it restores is real rather than merely quiet.

s: Depth of focus — Scorpio is read as the sign that goes past the surface of a person or situation by instinct.
Resilience — the archetype is described as capable of regenerating after loss or crisis rather than merely enduring it.
Loyalty once given — Scorpio's commitment, the tradition holds, is total and not easily withdrawn.
Perceptiveness — the pattern notices what is unsaid, reading motive beneath the stated reason.
Fixed intensity — as a fixed water sign, its feeling is read as holding its shape under pressure rather than dispersing.
d: Secrecy — Scorpio withholds by default, the tradition notes, disclosing only on its own terms.
Jealousy and control — the archetype's attachment is read as wanting certainty over the bond, not merely the bond itself.
Suspicion — Scorpio is described as assuming the hidden motive before the stated one.
All-or-nothing loyalty — the pattern reads betrayal as unforgivable, the tradition notes, closing rather than repairing.
Scorpio.m: What Scorpio is said to want is truth beneath the surface — the real motive, the real stakes — and it treats anything offered at half-depth as not offered at all.
Scorpio.g: The classic counsel for Scorpio is trust extended before proof arrives — the tradition reads its maturation as learning that control is not the only route to safety, and that a bond can survive being seen.
Scorpio.l: In relationship, Scorpio is described as intense, committed, and demanding of emotional honesty above ease. The archetype is said to bond completely once it trusts, and to treat lukewarm affection as an insult to the bond. Where the pattern is read to strain is in the pull toward control — testing loyalty rather than simply receiving it.
Scorpio.es: Scorpio is read as the sign of the undisguised depth — the refusal to accept the surface account of anything, power, death, and desire included.
gi: Intensity. Scorpio is credited with a concentration that does not dilute — once fixed on an object, the sign's attention the tradition holds rarely wanders.
Perception. Read as possessing an instinct for what is hidden or withheld — the tradition's account of the sign's diagnostic read on other people's motives.
Resilience. Credited with a capacity to survive its own ruin and emerge altered rather than diminished — the scorpion's proverbial recovery.
Fidelity. The tradition reads Scorpio's commitments, once made, as total — a loyalty that does not admit half-measures.
Scorpio.ha: The recurring difficulty the tradition assigns Scorpio is the same intensity turned suspicious — trust, once broken, is rarely extended twice, and the sign's own guardedness can seal off the very intimacy it seeks.
Scorpio.fr: As friend and ally, Scorpio is described as fiercely private but total once admitted — the tradition holds few are let past the sign's guard, but those who are receive an unqualified loyalty rarely offered elsewhere.

va.label: First decan (23 Oct – 1 Nov)
va.note: Ruled by Mars — Scorpio's own decan, the sign's intensity at its least mediated.

va.label: Second decan (2–11 Nov)
va.note: Ruled by the Sun — the decan the tradition reads as Scorpio's will made most visible, power less concealed.

va.label: Third decan (12–21 Nov)
va.note: Ruled by Venus — the decan tempering Scorpio's depth with attachment, intensity directed toward another rather than held alone.

Scorpio.wk: The Scorpio pattern is read as built for the work that goes past the surface — the single object fixed on and pursued to its real depth, its buried motive or stakes brought up rather than accepted at face value. Its concentration does not dilute, the tradition holds; once the attention settles it rarely wanders, and it can survive its own ruin to return altered. Work that calls for total, undivided focus suits it; work that asks it to stay light and skate the top is foreign to the pattern.
Scorpio.cr: Crossed, the Scorpio pattern is read as going quiet and reading beneath the stated reason for the real one — guarding, testing the bond rather than simply voicing the wound. The tradition holds its hardest repair is trust extended before proof arrives, since a betrayal it judges real tends to close the door rather than mend it. What mends it is being allowed to be seen without being controlled, and a loyalty that answers its intensity in kind.

s: Horizon-seeking — Sagittarius is read as the sign of the long view, restless for the next country, the next book, the next idea that widens the frame.
Blunt honesty — the tradition credits it with saying the thing plainly, treating tact as a delay rather than a virtue.
Buoyant under pressure — Jupiter's expansiveness is ascribed to it as an instinct to find the larger meaning in a setback rather than sit inside it.
Generous scale of thought — Sagittarius is read as a philosopher-archer, aiming past the immediate target toward doctrine, belief, the whole system.
d: Overshoot — the same arrow that clears the near obstacle is read as prone to missing the actual mark, promising more than gets delivered.
Tactlessness — bluntness curdles, in the tradition's account, into a bluntness that does not check whether the truth was wanted.
Commitment aversion — routine and confinement are read as the sign's chief discomforts, with steady arrangements treated as a cage to be tested at the edges.
Restlessness mistaken for growth — the tradition notes a tendency to equate motion itself with progress, leaving projects and places behind before they finish.
Sagittarius.m: What Sagittarius is said to want, above other things, is expanse — more territory, more doctrine, more sky — and it measures a life by how much of the world it has covered rather than how deeply it has stayed in one place.
Sagittarius.g: The classic counsel for Sagittarius is depth without abandoning breadth — the tradition reads its maturation as learning that a single terrain, examined closely, holds as much horizon as the next country over.
Sagittarius.l: In relationship, Sagittarius is described as a companion who needs room to leave and return, treating a partner more as a fellow traveler than a fixed post. The archetype is read as generous and honest to a fault, offering enthusiasm and adventure readily, while withholding the settled promises that make a partner feel anchored. Its pattern in partnership is said to soften only when the bond itself is framed as another open road rather than a closed door.
Sagittarius.es: Sagittarius is the mutable fire sign of the zodiac's third quarter, the archer aimed past the visible target — motion organized around a horizon rather than a destination.
gi: Distance-crossing. The tradition reads Sagittarius as the sign that treats a border as an invitation rather than a stop, extending the map before the territory has been settled.
Bluntness. Sagittarius is read as the sign that states the unwelcome fact plainly, on the premise that a true statement outranks a comfortable silence.
Meaning-making. The tradition assigns Sagittarius the task of compressing scattered experience into a working philosophy others can carry.
Buoyancy. Sagittarius is credited with restoring nerve to a discouraged room, less by argument than by visibly expecting things to come out well.
Sagittarius.ha: The tradition holds that the same reach that crosses borders struggles to stay inside one, and Sagittarius is read as accumulating unfinished frontiers — abandoned courses, outrun promises — faster than it accumulates completions.
Sagittarius.fr: As an ally, Sagittarius is read as the friend who arrives with an itinerary and leaves the consensus for later, prized for enthusiasm supplied on demand and, by the tradition's own account, unreliable for anything requiring a fixed date.

va.label: 1st decan (Nov 22 – Dec 1) — Sagittarius decan, ruled by Jupiter
va.note: The tradition assigns Jupiter's own rulership here, reading the decan as Sagittarius at its most unmixed: expansive, blunt, undiluted.

va.label: 2nd decan (Dec 2 – Dec 11) — Aries decan, sub-ruled by Mars
va.note: Mars is named sub-ruler of the second decan, and the tradition reads a harder edge in it, the archer's aim sharpened toward confrontation.

va.label: 3rd decan (Dec 12 – Dec 21) — Leo decan, sub-ruled by the Sun
va.note: The Sun governs the third decan by tradition, lending the closing days of Sagittarius a more performative, self-assured cast.

Sagittarius.wk: The Sagittarius pattern is read as built for the frontier — the task aimed past the visible target toward the wider system, the next terrain, the meaning that ties scattered experience together. Its labor is expansive and buoyant, the tradition holds, restoring nerve to a discouraged room by visibly expecting things to come out well. The difficulty assigned to it is the reach that struggles to stay inside one border: it accumulates opened frontiers — outrun promises, abandoned courses — faster than completions.
Sagittarius.cr: In conflict the Sagittarius pattern is read as blunt — stating the unwelcome fact plainly, on the premise that a true statement outranks a comfortable silence, without always checking whether the truth was wanted. The tradition holds it does not hold the quarrel long: its buoyancy pulls it back toward the larger view and the fresh start. What mends it is honesty met with honesty and a repair framed as another open road rather than a ledger of the wrong.

s: Structural patience — Capricorn is read as the sign that builds for decades, laying foundation before ornament.
Discipline as instinct — Saturn's rule is ascribed to it as an internal taskmaster that treats effort as owed rather than optional.
Reliability under scrutiny — the tradition credits Capricorn with meeting responsibility calmly, the figure others hand the hard job to.
Long-game ambition — status and achievement are read as pursued methodically, rung by rung, with the summit kept in view across years.
d: Joylessness — the tradition notes a tendency to defer pleasure so long that the goal itself is arrived at hollow.
Rigidity — structure hardens, in the classic account, into an unwillingness to bend the plan even when the plan is wrong.
Emotional reserve — Capricorn is read as guarded with feeling, treating vulnerability as a liability to be managed rather than shared.
Status fixation — achievement can be mistaken, the tradition warns, for worth itself, leaving the climb unable to stop even at the top.
Capricorn.m: What Capricorn is said to want, before recognition or comfort, is mastery earned the hard way — a structure built by its own labor that cannot be taken away because it was never given.
Capricorn.g: The classic counsel for Capricorn is that the summit is not the self — the tradition reads its maturation as learning to let achievement rest on the ground it built rather than carry the whole identity on its back.
Capricorn.l: In relationship, Capricorn is described as slow to commit and serious once committed, testing a bond's durability before trusting it with feeling. The archetype is read as loyal, practical, and quietly protective, expressing care through provision and consistency more than through display. Its pattern in partnership is said to soften only when it learns that tenderness is not a weakness the structure must be defended against.
Capricorn.es: Capricorn is the cardinal earth sign that opens winter, read as the archetype that treats limitation as a material to build with rather than a wall to mourn.
gi: Load-bearing patience. The tradition credits Capricorn with carrying the long, unglamorous middle stretch of a project without needing applause to continue.
Structural clarity. Capricorn is read as the sign that supplies the schedule, the hierarchy, and the terms once everyone else has finished admiring the idea.
Earned authority. The tradition ascribes to Capricorn a gravity that accumulates rather than arrives, so its word carries weight in rooms it has quietly outlasted.
Dry humor. Capricorn is credited with an understated wit that surfaces once the work is finished, functioning as relief rather than performance.
Capricorn.ha: The tradition holds that Capricorn's climb leaves little accounted for beyond what the climb required, and the sign is read as prone to mistaking exhaustion for virtue.
Capricorn.fr: As a friend, Capricorn is read as steady rather than expressive — present at the practical moment, reliable across years, and slow to state directly what it means by loyalty.

va.label: 1st decan (Dec 22 – 31) — Capricorn decan, ruled by Saturn
va.note: Saturn rules the first decan by its own sign, read as Capricorn concentrated: the most disciplined and least sentimental face of the sign.

va.label: 2nd decan (Jan 1 – 10) — Taurus decan, sub-ruled by Venus
va.note: Venus is named sub-ruler of the second decan, and the tradition reads a softened, more sensual patience here, ambition pursued through comfort rather than austerity.

va.label: 3rd decan (Jan 11 – 19) — Virgo decan, sub-ruled by Mercury
va.note: Mercury governs the third decan by tradition, sharpening the closing days into precision and a more analytical bent.

Capricorn.wk: The Capricorn pattern is read as built for the long structural haul — foundation laid before ornament, the unglamorous middle stretch of a task carried without needing applause to continue. Its discipline is read as internal rather than imposed, treating sustained effort as owed rather than optional, and its patience as the kind that works a thing rung by rung across years. The difficulty the tradition assigns it is a climb that leaves little accounted for beyond what the climb required, and a tendency to mistake exhaustion for virtue.
Capricorn.cr: Crossed, the Capricorn pattern is read as going reserved rather than expressive — managing the feeling as a liability to be contained rather than voicing it, defending the structure against being seen. The tradition holds its repair arrives through consistency and quiet provision, the steady return rather than the stated apology. What mends it is learning that tenderness is not a weakness the structure must be guarded from, and a counterpart who reads its constancy as the care it is.

s: Original thought — Aquarius is read as the sign least willing to inherit an idea unexamined, testing convention before accepting it.
Collective orientation — the tradition ascribes to it a concern for the group, the cause, the system, over the purely personal stake.
Fixed conviction — once a principle is adopted, Aquarius is described as holding it with a stubbornness that outlasts social pressure.
Future-facing vision — Uranus's rule is read as an instinct to see past the present arrangement toward what could replace it.
d: Detachment — the tradition notes a coolness toward individual feeling, its own and others', in service of the larger principle.
Contrarianism for its own sake — unconventionality can curdle, in the classic account, into difference performed rather than meant.
Stubborn abstraction — fixed ideas are read as capable of outlasting their usefulness, held past the point the evidence has moved on.
Aloofness read as coldness — the same distance that lets Aquarius see the whole system is described as leaving intimacy feeling withheld.
Aquarius.m: What Aquarius is said to want, more than closeness or approval, is a better system — a world rearranged along more rational or humane lines, with itself as one node serving that larger pattern.
Aquarius.g: The classic counsel for Aquarius is that the near person also counts — the tradition reads its maturation as learning that the individual in front of it is not a smaller case of the collective but a claim in its own right.
Aquarius.l: In relationship, Aquarius is described as a partner who prizes friendship and independence over conventional romantic display, often approaching intimacy sideways rather than head-on. The archetype is read as loyal in its own register, showing devotion through ideas shared and causes joined rather than through declared feeling. Its pattern in partnership is said to open only when the bond is permitted to remain a little unconventional, unpinned by expectation.
Aquarius.es: Aquarius is the fixed air sign of the zodiac's final quarter, read as loyal to the pattern it has chosen for itself ahead of any bond of blood or crowd.
gi: Structural imagination. The tradition credits Aquarius with picturing an arrangement nobody else has tried, then defending it with the same fixity a Taurus reserves for what already exists.
Detachment as instrument. Aquarius is read as able to step back from an outcome it cares about, a distancing the tradition treats as clarity rather than coldness.
Collective framing. The tradition ascribes to Aquarius the habit of asking what serves the group before what serves the individual case in front of it.
Unbothered originality. Aquarius is credited with holding a minority position without requiring it be ratified — the tradition's most literal reading of the sign's fixed-air constitution.
Aquarius.ha: The tradition holds that the same distance that grants Aquarius its clarity keeps it slow to register the ordinary, un-abstracted needs of whoever stands closest to it.
Aquarius.fr: As a friend, Aquarius is read as loyal to the idea of the friendship as much as to any one person inside it, offering camaraderie freely while withholding the more intimate register until trust has been tested.

va.label: 1st decan (Jan 20 – 29) — Aquarius decan, ruled by Saturn
va.note: Saturn, Aquarius's traditional ruler, governs the first decan, read as the sign at its most detached and its most fixed.

va.label: 2nd decan (Jan 30 – Feb 8) — Gemini decan, sub-ruled by Mercury
va.note: Mercury is named sub-ruler of the second decan, and the tradition reads a lighter, more sociable and talkative Aquarius here.

va.label: 3rd decan (Feb 9 – 18) — Libra decan, sub-ruled by Venus
va.note: Venus governs the third decan by tradition, softening the closing days toward partnership and a more aesthetic idealism.

Aquarius.wk: The Aquarius pattern is read as built for the untried arrangement — the task of picturing a system nobody has attempted and then holding to it with the same fixity another sign reserves for what already exists. Its labor is principled and detached, the tradition holds, able to step back from an outcome it cares about and hold a minority position without needing it ratified. Work that asks it to inherit a convention unexamined is where the pattern balks; work that lets it redesign the whole keeps it committed.
Aquarius.cr: In conflict the Aquarius pattern is read as arguing from principle rather than feeling — cool toward the individual stake, its own and others', in service of the larger point. The tradition holds the same distance that grants its clarity can leave it slow to register the plain hurt of whoever stands closest. What mends it is turning that attention back to the near person as a claim in its own right rather than a smaller case of the collective, and a counterpart who grants it room to remain a little unconventional.

s: Boundless empathy — Pisces is read as the sign most willing to absorb another's feeling as though it were its own.
Imaginative reach — Neptune's rule is ascribed to it as a native fluency with image, dream, and symbol, the last sign closing the wheel by dissolving its edges.
Compassion without condition — the tradition credits Pisces with mercy extended before judgment, forgiveness offered ahead of any account being settled.
Creative and spiritual sensitivity — art and the numinous are read as native languages, arrived at without needing to be taught.
d: Boundary dissolution — the same permeability that allows empathy is read as an inability to say clearly where the self ends and another begins.
Escapism — Pisces is described as prone to retreat into fantasy, substance, or story rather than remain inside an unpleasant present.
Susceptibility to being overwhelmed — others' emotion is absorbed so readily, the tradition notes, that its own feeling can go untracked underneath the flood.
Martyrdom — self-sacrifice can tip, in the classic account, into a suffering performed and half-invited rather than simply borne.
Pisces.m: What Pisces is said to want, beneath particular attachments, is dissolution of the separateness itself — union with something larger, whether another person, a art, or the unseen, that lets the boundary of the self go soft.
Pisces.g: The classic counsel for Pisces is a boundary that does not require becoming hard — the tradition reads its maturation as learning to stay permeable without disappearing into what it absorbs.
Pisces.l: In relationship, Pisces is described as a partner who merges readily, often experiencing the other's mood as directly as its own. The archetype is read as tender, romantic, and forgiving past the point that self-protection would suggest, drawn to rescue or be rescued. Its pattern in partnership is said to steady only when some part of it is kept apart, undissolved, able to notice where it ends and the other begins.
Pisces.es: Pisces is the mutable water sign that closes the zodiac, read as the point where every prior sign's boundary dissolves back into a single undifferentiated current.
gi: Permeability. The tradition credits Pisces with absorbing what a room actually feels, ahead of what it says, treating the sensitivity as instrument rather than weakness.
Imaginative range. Pisces is read as the sign least bound by the literal, able to hold a symbol, a myth, or an unfinished sketch as seriously as a fact.
Compassion without condition. The tradition ascribes to Pisces a mercy extended before the object of it has been assessed as deserving.
Artistic transmission. Pisces is credited with translating feeling directly into image or sound, skipping the argument other signs require first.
Pisces.ha: The tradition holds that the boundary Pisces dissolves in others, it also dissolves in itself, and the sign is read as chronically underdefended against what it absorbs.
Pisces.fr: As a friend, Pisces is read as the one who meets a person's mood before their words, offering an unusually deep sympathy that the tradition also flags as difficult to relocate once the mood has passed.

va.label: 1st decan (Feb 19 – 29) — Pisces decan, ruled by Jupiter
va.note: Jupiter, Pisces's traditional ruler, governs the first decan, read as the sign at its most diffuse and its most generous.

va.label: 2nd decan (Mar 1 – 10) — Scorpio decan, sub-ruled by Mars
va.note: Mars is named sub-ruler of the second decan by tradition (Scorpio's classical ruler), lending the middle days a more intense, undertow quality.

va.label: 3rd decan (Mar 11 – 20) — Cancer decan, sub-ruled by the Moon
va.note: The Moon governs the third decan by tradition, drawing the closing days of Pisces toward memory, home, and a more inward-facing sensitivity.

Pisces.wk: The Pisces pattern is read as built for the work of transmission — feeling turned directly into image or sound, the symbol and the unfinished sketch held as seriously as a fact. Its labor is permeable and imaginative, the tradition holds, absorbing what a room actually feels ahead of what it says and skipping the argument other signs require first. The difficulty assigned to it is the boundary it dissolves in others dissolving in itself, so it is read as chronically underdefended against what it takes in.
Pisces.cr: Crossed, the Pisces pattern is read as absorbing rather than asserting — taking on the other's mood so directly that its own hurt goes untracked beneath the flood, sometimes retreating into fantasy or a half-invited suffering rather than naming the grievance. The tradition holds the repair it needs is a boundary that does not require becoming hard: some part of it kept apart, undissolved, able to notice where it ends and the other begins. What mends it is being met with a clarity it can borrow when its own edges have gone soft.

s: Quick assessment — the Rat is read as the first to spot an opening and the first to move toward it.
Resourcefulness — tradition credits the Rat with making a working solution out of whatever is on hand.
Social intelligence — the Rat is described as fluent in reading a room and positioning itself within it.
Thrift — the sign is associated with careful accumulation, storing against a winter not yet visible.
d: Hoarding — the accumulation the Rat is praised for is also read as an inability to spend or release.
Restlessness — the Rat's alertness is said to tip into an inability to settle on one commitment.
Suspicion — the same reading eye that spots opportunity is described as quick to suspect a trap.
Opportunism without loyalty — tradition notes the Rat can trade an old alliance for a better-looking new one.
Rat.m: The Rat archetype is organized around security obtained through wit rather than force — the tradition holds that survival, not conquest, is the animal's central concern.
Rat.g: The classic counsel for the Rat is that not every gain needs to be banked — the tradition reads its maturation as learning to release a stockpile once the need that built it has passed.
Rat.l: The Rat is described in partnership as attentive and quick to notice what a partner needs before it is asked for. Tradition also reads the Rat as slow to be fully transparent, keeping some reserve even in closeness. Its affection is said to be shown through provision and problem-solving rather than declaration.
Rat.es: The Rat opens the twelve-year cycle and is read as the archetype of first movement — quick assessment of a closed room, and the resourcefulness to enter it before the room has finished deciding whether it is open.
gi: Foresight: the Rat is credited with seeing the gap before it opens and moving into it first.
Adaptability: the tradition holds that no terrain remains foreign to the Rat for long.
Thrift: an instinct assigned to the Rat for conserving what others discard as spent.
Wit: fast, unshowy reading of a room, treated as the Rat's native intelligence rather than a learned skill.
Rat.ha: The tradition also records a restlessness in the Rat that resists settling once the immediate opening has been secured, and a suspicion that arrives well ahead of trust and is slow to leave once trust is offered.
Rat.fr: The Rat is read as bonding through usefulness and shared advantage rather than sentiment, closest to the Dragon and the Monkey, and holding a guarded but genuine loyalty to the Ox — its opposite in temperament, and its named ally in the founding race that ordered the cycle.

va.label: Wood Rat (1924, 1984)
va.note: Wood is read as softening the Rat's calculation into cooperation, the resourcefulness turned toward building alongside others rather than around them.

va.label: Fire Rat (1936, 1996)
va.note: Fire is read as sharpening the Rat's quickness into visible ambition, the first-mover instinct made bold rather than discreet.

va.label: Earth Rat (1948, 2008)
va.note: Earth is read as anchoring the Rat's restlessness, the tradition noting a steadier, more provident version of the sign.

va.label: Metal Rat (1900, 1960, 2020)
va.note: Metal is read as hardening the Rat's thrift into a sharper acquisitiveness, ambition given an edge and a fixed target.

va.label: Water Rat (1912, 1972)
va.note: Water is read as deepening the Rat's adaptability into fluency, the sign at its most socially agile and least fixed in position.

Rat.wk: The Rat is read as working by opening rather than by plan — spotting the gap before it widens and improvising a solution out of whatever the room has left lying around. The tradition credits it with speed and resourcefulness at the start, and notes a restlessness that resists settling into the long tending of what the opening began; it is held to be built for the quick fix and the scramble, not the patient watch that follows.
Rat.cr: Crossed, the Rat is read as quick and evasive — reading a dispute for its exits before committing to one, and quicker still to suspect a trap laid inside the other's words. The tradition holds the breach mends when the pattern lets someone past that first calculation, spending the reserve it keeps even in closeness rather than trading an old bond for a better-looking position.

s: Endurance — the Ox is read as the animal that finishes what it starts, regardless of how long the task takes.
Reliability — tradition holds the Ox's word and labor as fixed points others are said to build plans around.
Methodical patience — the Ox is described as working a problem in order, without shortcuts.
Quiet strength — the sign is associated with a capacity carried without display or complaint.
d: Rigidity — the Ox's steadiness is also read as an unwillingness to adjust a plan once it is set.
Stubbornness — tradition notes the Ox can mistake persistence in error for integrity.
Emotional withholding — the same quiet strength is described as a reluctance to voice distress until it is severe.
Slow to forgive — the Ox is said to hold an old grievance with the same steadiness it holds everything else.
Ox.m: The Ox archetype is organized around duty carried to completion — the tradition holds that worth, for this sign, is demonstrated through sustained labor rather than through speed or display.
Ox.g: The classic counsel for the Ox is flexibility it did not request — the tradition reads its maturation as learning that changing course under new information is not the same as abandoning the work.
Ox.l: The Ox is described in partnership as steady and slow to waver once committed. Tradition reads its affection as expressed through consistent action — showing up, providing, remaining — more than through words. The same reading notes the Ox can be slow to voice needs, expecting steadiness to be read as devotion on its own.
Ox.es: The Ox is read as the archetype of sustained labor — patience exercised across a long season, in contrast to the single decisive act, and the sign the tradition trusts to still be present when the work is not yet finished.
gi: Endurance: the Ox is credited with carrying a load past the point where other signs set it down.
Reliability: the tradition holds that the Ox's word and the Ox's presence arrive on schedule, without renegotiation.
Groundedness: a steadying quality ascribed to the Ox in rooms otherwise given to haste.
Reserve of force: strength the tradition treats as held in reserve rather than displayed.
Ox.ha: The tradition also records a stubbornness in the Ox that resists redirection even after the original path has closed, and a reluctance to ask for aid that leaves its labor carried unwitnessed.
Ox.fr: The Ox is read as slow to bond and near-permanent once the bond is formed, closest to the Rat and the Rooster, and regarding the Sheep, its opposite, with a wary but not hostile distance.

va.label: Wood Ox (1925, 1985)
va.note: Wood is read as loosening the Ox's fixity, the endurance directed toward growth and gradual expansion rather than mere upkeep.

va.label: Fire Ox (1937, 1997)
va.note: Fire is read as giving the Ox's steadiness a visible drive, labor performed with an intensity the sign does not usually show.

va.label: Earth Ox (1949, 2009)
va.note: Earth is read as doubling the Ox's native groundedness, the tradition's most conservative and most dependable reading of the sign.

va.label: Metal Ox (1901, 1961, 2021)
va.note: Metal is read as hardening the Ox's stubbornness into an uncompromising will, reliability turned rigid.

va.label: Water Ox (1913, 1973)
va.note: Water is read as softening the Ox's rigidity, endurance paired with an unusual patience for the moods of others.

Ox.wk: The Ox is read as working in order and without shortcuts — carrying a load past the point where other signs set it down, and finishing what it starts regardless of how long the task runs. The tradition holds it slow to begin moving and nearly impossible to stop once moving, its worth demonstrated through duration rather than speed; it is read as built for the long, unglamorous stretch that outlasts the urgency around it.
Ox.cr: Crossed, the Ox is read as immovable rather than loud — it plants a position and waits, holding a grievance with the same steadiness it holds a task, and slow to voice the distress underneath. The tradition reads the repair as slow by nature: the breach closes when the pattern accepts that bending to new information is not the abandonment of the work, and when the withheld hurt is finally named.

s: Boldness — the Tiger is read as the animal that moves first and asks permission after, if at all.
Charisma — tradition credits the Tiger with a magnetism that draws others to follow without being asked.
Courage under pressure — the sign is associated with steadiness precisely when stakes rise.
Independence — the Tiger is described as capable of acting alone, unbothered by the absence of consensus.
d: Impulsiveness — the boldness ascribed to the Tiger is also read as action taken before consequence is weighed.
Impatience with authority — tradition notes the Tiger chafes against instruction it did not choose.
Volatility — the same intensity that reads as courage is described as a temper quick to surface.
Restlessness under confinement — the Tiger is said to treat any cage, literal or procedural, as an insult.
Tiger.m: The Tiger archetype is organized around autonomy and the exercise of will — the tradition holds that the animal's central concern is command of its own direction, not the approval that command may or may not bring.
Tiger.g: The classic counsel for the Tiger is restraint it did not ask for — the tradition reads its maturation as learning that withheld action can be a form of strength rather than a defeat.
Tiger.l: The Tiger is described in partnership as intense and openly declarative, arriving without half-measures. Tradition reads the pattern as needing a partner who neither cowers before its force nor tries to leash it outright. Its affection is said to be as consuming as its temper, offered fully or not at all.
Tiger.es: The Tiger is read as the archetype of open confrontation — courage that acts before permission is granted, and the sign the tradition assigns to guard the threshold rather than wait behind it.
gi: Boldness: the Tiger is credited with entering the room others are still deciding whether to enter.
Unrequested authority: a natural command the tradition ascribes to the Tiger's presence, given rather than asked for.
Protection: the Tiger read as guardian, first to place itself between a threat and the unguarded.
Vitality: an unspent charge of energy the tradition treats as the Tiger's baseline state, not its exception.
Tiger.ha: The tradition also records an impatience with process in the Tiger, and a tendency toward isolation, since the same boldness that leads a room also tends to outpace the room it leads.
Tiger.fr: The Tiger is read as forming few but intense and loyal bonds rather than many casual ones, closest to the Horse and the Dog, and set in the tradition's oldest recorded rivalry with the Monkey.

va.label: Wood Tiger (1914, 1974)
va.note: Wood is read as giving the Tiger's boldness direction and growth, courage put toward building rather than only breaking through.

va.label: Fire Tiger (1926, 1986)
va.note: Fire is read as intensifying the Tiger to its most combustible reading, boldness with little patience for restraint.

va.label: Earth Tiger (1938, 1998)
va.note: Earth is read as grounding the Tiger's charge, authority exercised with more deliberation and less impulse.

va.label: Metal Tiger (1950, 2010)
va.note: Metal is read as sharpening the Tiger's protective instinct into decisiveness, guardianship with a cutting edge.

va.label: Water Tiger (1902, 1962, 2022)
va.note: Water is read as tempering the Tiger's confrontation with adaptability, boldness that reads the room before it enters.

Tiger.wk: The Tiger is read as working in charges — entering before the room has decided to, acting on its own will rather than any consensus, an unspent energy treated as its baseline rather than its exception. The tradition holds it built for the bold opening and the guarded threshold, and impatient with the procedural middle; what it ignites through sheer nerve, it is read as having little patience to administer.
Tiger.cr: Crossed, the Tiger is read as meeting a fight head-on and at full voice — its temper the first thing in the room and its charge aimed before the cost is weighed. The tradition holds the breach mends when the same force is briefly withheld, restraint offered as the harder strength, and when the room it outpaced is allowed to catch up rather than be run over.

s: Diplomacy — the Rabbit is read as the animal that finds the path through conflict rather than across it.
Discernment — tradition credits the Rabbit with a fine sense for danger well before it arrives.
Grace under scrutiny — the sign is associated with composure that does not crack under a difficult audience.
Cultivated taste — the Rabbit is described as drawn toward refinement and comfort as matters of principle.
d: Avoidance — the diplomacy ascribed to the Rabbit is also read as a tendency to sidestep confrontation indefinitely.
Overcaution — tradition notes the Rabbit's danger-sense can harden into refusal to risk anything at all.
Withdrawal — the same composure is described as retreat behind a closed door when pressure mounts.
Indecision — the Rabbit is said to weigh comfort against comfort long past the point of a usable choice.
Rabbit.m: The Rabbit archetype is organized around the preservation of peace and comfort — the tradition holds that safety, aesthetic and circumstantial alike, is this sign's governing concern.
Rabbit.g: The classic counsel for the Rabbit is direct confrontation it did not seek out — the tradition reads its maturation as learning that a difficult exchange, met early, costs less than the retreat that postpones it.
Rabbit.l: The Rabbit is described in partnership as gentle, accommodating, and attentive to a partner's comfort. Tradition reads the pattern as prone to withholding its own complaint to keep the peace intact. Its affection is said to be shown through the quiet maintenance of a shared, comfortable life rather than through open declaration.
Rabbit.es: The Rabbit is read as the archetype of tact — conflict avoided by skill rather than conflict never arising, and the sign the tradition trusts to keep a room comfortable without anyone noticing the work of keeping it so.
gi: Diplomacy: the Rabbit is credited with finding the exit from a quarrel before the quarrel is named.
Discretion: a native gift for privacy, and for holding what should not be spread.
Aesthetic sense: the tradition assigns the Rabbit an eye for refinement and comfort that others overlook.
Composure: an outward calm the tradition treats as armor, not the absence of feeling.
Rabbit.ha: The tradition also records a conflict-avoidance in the Rabbit that can curdle into evasiveness, and a private worry kept so well hidden that it goes untended even by the Rabbit itself.
Rabbit.fr: The Rabbit is read as bonding through comfort and mutual courtesy rather than intensity, closest to the Sheep and the Pig, and maintaining a careful, formal distance from the Rooster, its opposite in the cycle.

va.label: Wood Rabbit (1915, 1975)
va.note: Wood is read as extending the Rabbit's tact into genuine warmth, diplomacy paired with steady growth in its circle.

va.label: Fire Rabbit (1927, 1987)
va.note: Fire is read as giving the Rabbit's composure an unusual warmth and visibility, tact practiced with more open charm.

va.label: Earth Rabbit (1939, 1999)
va.note: Earth is read as the most settled reading of the sign, discretion paired with domestic steadiness.

va.label: Metal Rabbit (1951, 2011)
va.note: Metal is read as giving the Rabbit's tact a firmer edge, diplomacy that holds a line rather than only smoothing it over.

va.label: Water Rabbit (1903, 1963, 2023)
va.note: Water is read as deepening the Rabbit's discretion into intuition, the sign most attuned to what a room is not saying.

Rabbit.wk: The Rabbit is read as working quietly and out of sight — keeping a room comfortable without anyone noticing the labor of keeping it so, and sensing a difficulty well before it arrives. The tradition credits it with composure as method and an eye for refinement others overlook; it is held to be built for the delicate task and the unshowy maintenance, not the forced push.
Rabbit.cr: Crossed, the Rabbit is read as declining the fight rather than waging it — smoothing, deflecting, or withdrawing behind a closed door before a voice is raised. The tradition reads the repair as the harder turn toward directness: the postponed exchange is held to cost more than the difficult one met early, and the breach mends only when the withheld complaint is finally spoken.

s: Sky-borne authority — the Dragon is the one figure in the cycle not drawn from an earthly animal, and the tradition reads it as commanding presence by default, not by effort.
Storm-caller's momentum — classical association with thunder, rain, and spring gives the Dragon a charge of arriving force, read as luck that moves rather than luck that waits.
Cosmological rank — paired with the Tiger as sky to earth in the I Ching, the Dragon is read as the higher register of the two, the one native to ascent.
Magnetism without petition — the Dragon is described as drawing attention and following without asking for either.
d: Untethered scale — a figure with no earthly counterpart is read as prone to operating above the room, mistaking altitude for accuracy.
Storm as habit — the same thunder that announces arrival is read as a temperament that can flood a situation it only meant to enter.
Rank assumed, not earned — cosmological seniority in the pairing with Tiger is read as a tendency to expect deference before it is demonstrated.
Isolation at the top — a sign with singular position in the cycle is read as one that can crowd out the ordinary give and take a partnership requires.
Dragon.m: The Dragon pattern is read as oriented toward ascent and consequence — the tradition holds that what drives it is the difference its arrival makes, not comfort or consensus.
Dragon.g: The classic counsel for the Dragon pattern is descent by choice — the tradition reads its maturing as learning to touch ordinary ground without treating it as a demotion.
Dragon.l: In partnership, the Dragon is read as arriving fully formed, offering weather rather than negotiation. The tradition describes it as most workable for a partner able to meet scale with scale rather than being flooded by it. Where the pattern is said to soften, it is described as learning that a quieter season is not an absence of the storm.
Dragon.es: The Dragon is read as the animal that arrives already ahead of the room, the sign the tradition assigns to command that requires no permission. It is the only mythical creature in the cycle, and the lore treats that fact as load-bearing rather than decorative.
gi: Command. The Dragon is read as the animal a room defers to before it has spoken, the way weather is deferred to.
Largeness of scale. Where other signs are counseled to be reasonable, the Dragon is the one the tradition permits to think in centuries and empires.
Luck as inheritance. Of the twelve, the Dragon alone is held to be born already favored, fortune arriving as birthright rather than as reward.
Unconcealed conviction. The Dragon is read as unable to fake modesty convincingly, and the tradition does not ask it to.
Dragon.ha: The hardship the tradition assigns the Dragon is isolation at altitude: a presence too large for ordinary rooms tends to empty them. Where it is not obeyed, the lore holds it is often simply avoided.
Dragon.fr: The Dragon is traditionally paired with the Rat and the Monkey, both quick enough to keep pace without needing to be led. It bonds poorly with those who require constant reassurance, offering loyalty instead of comfort.

va.label: Wood Dragon (1904, 1964, 2024)
va.note: The tradition reads this as the most flexible of the five, command tempered by a willingness to be corrected.

va.label: Fire Dragon (1916, 1976)
va.note: Held to be the most volatile pairing, the animal's native heat doubled rather than balanced.

va.label: Earth Dragon (1928, 1988)
va.note: Read as the most grounded form, ambition given patience it does not naturally possess.

va.label: Metal Dragon (1940, 2000)
va.note: The tradition assigns this form unbending resolve, command that does not negotiate.

va.label: Water Dragon (1952, 2012)
va.note: Read as the most diplomatic variant, force carried underneath rather than announced.

Dragon.wk: The Dragon is read as working at scale and from above — arriving already ahead of the room and commanding a task rather than negotiating its way into it. The tradition holds it brings weather rather than incremental effort, its presence doing the work other signs manage by degrees; it is read as built for the large arrival and ill-suited to the patient, ground-level tending that follows.
Dragon.cr: Crossed, the Dragon is read as bringing weather to a disagreement — the storm that announces it can flood a room it only meant to enter, and it is read as expecting deference before the matter is even argued. The tradition holds the breach closes through descent by choice: the pattern meeting the other at ordinary ground rather than above it, and treating that descent as no demotion.

s: Intuitive read of a room — classical sources emphasize intelligence and intuition as the Snake's core register, ahead of any social display.
Economy of motion — the Great Race legend has the Snake advancing by a single well-timed move rather than sustained effort, read as a pattern that prefers precision to exertion.
Patient accumulation — the Snake is read as building position quietly, letting a situation mature before it acts within it.
Discretion as method — where other signs announce, the Snake is read as working beneath notice by design, not by accident.
d: Trickery on record — the folk legend has the Snake gaining ground by startling the Horse it rode in on, and the tradition reads this as a willingness to use surprise rather than declared intent.
Reserve read as concealment — the same discretion that serves it is read, in the harsher modern gloss, as secretiveness or suspicion toward those outside its calculation.
Compensation over parity — the twentieth-century 'Little Dragon' relabeling of Snake years is read as evidence of a pattern anxious about its own standing in the cycle.
Coiled rather than open — the Snake's economy of motion is read, in shadow, as withholding — giving less of itself than a situation actually asked for.
Snake.m: The Snake pattern is read as oriented toward accurate timing — the tradition holds that what it is after is the single correct moment, not visible activity.
Snake.g: The classic counsel for the Snake pattern is disclosure ahead of proof — the tradition reads its maturing as learning to show a hand before the outcome has already vindicated it.
Snake.l: In partnership, the Snake is read as attentive in a register others miss, tracking undercurrents rather than declarations. The tradition describes its bond as built slowly, through accurate observation rather than display. Where the pattern is said to falter, it is described as answering a partner's directness with calculation instead of the same directness returned.
Snake.es: The Snake is read as intelligence that does not announce itself, the sign the tradition credits with knowing the outcome of the room before the room has finished talking. Where the Dragon rules by presence, the Snake is held to rule by information withheld.
gi: Discernment. The Snake is read as the animal that notices the lie in a room before the lie has finished being told.
Composure. The tradition holds the Snake still while calculating, mistaking stillness for passivity being the classic misread.
Depth of counsel. Where advice from other signs is quick, the Snake's is traditionally slow, arriving only once, and arriving correct.
Private resourcefulness. The Snake is read as never depleted by scarcity, finding advantage in conditions others would call a shortage.
Snake.ha: The hardship the tradition assigns the Snake is a suspicion it invites simply by being unreadable; a mind this private is traditionally mistrusted regardless of its intentions. Sharing credit is held to be the pattern's harder discipline.
